text:
One of the biggest investment opportunities of this decade is quietly taking shape, and most investors still haven’t noticed.

Photonics today feels a lot like memory did a year ago. That’s exactly why we’re still early.

If you’ve followed the AI supercycle since 2022, the pattern is becoming impossible to ignore.

🚀 Phase 1: Chips

When ChatGPT changed the world, AI demand exploded. NVIDIA’s Hopper H100 and Blackwell GPUs became the backbone of AI infrastructure, while AMD steadily gained ground with its own GPUs and custom AI chips.

💾 Phase 2: Memory

As AI models grew larger, High Bandwidth Memory (HBM) became the next bottleneck. Demand surged, supply tightened, and companies like Micron, SK Hynix, and Samsung experienced powerful re-ratings as pricing power returned. Today, memory is in the middle of a prolonged supercycle, with capacity sold out years in advance.

💡 Phase 3: Photonics

Now the next bottleneck is emerging.

AI data centers are pushing against the limits of electricity, heat, bandwidth, and latency. Traditional electrical connections simply can’t keep up.

That’s where silicon photonics (SiPh) and co-packaged optics (CPO) come in.

Instead of moving data with electrical signals, they use light—making AI clusters faster, more efficient, and significantly more power-friendly.

The industry is only beginning this transition:

• 1.6T optical transceivers are entering production.
• Early CPO deployments have started.
• Large-scale adoption is expected in 2027–2028 and beyond.
• Goldman Sachs expects the market opportunity to grow roughly 10x by the end of the decade.

Looking at the AI supercycle through three stages:

📈 Chips → Mature Growth (Phase 3)
📈 Memory → Late Acceleration (Phase 2/3)
📈 Photonics → Early Acceleration (Phase 2) 🔥

Every AI wave creates a new bottleneck, and every bottleneck creates a new investment opportunity.

First it was compute.
Then it was memory.
Now it’s connectivity.

The world isn’t asking for less AI—it needs exponentially more compute, more bandwidth, and more efficiency.

That’s why photonics could become one of the defining investment themes of the second half of this decade.

At this point, calling this market “bullish” almost feels bearish.

text:
Our strategy must be smarter. Our true competitive advantage is our massive digital market with ~13 Billion Monthly UPI Transactions processing ~$240 Billion USD.
​This frictionless economy generates behavioral data of unprecedented quality. This data is our bargaining chip in the new negotiation technology landscape. Our goal is not a self-sufficient ecosystem, but an irreplaceable one in the global value chain.
Strategic Alliances are Key: We must stop relying on the west and build a warehouse of specialized non-US allies.
​🔗 Japan: Optical Computing & Advanced Photonics.
🔗 South Korea: High-Density Data Center Comms & Power Highways.
🔗 Taiwan: Advanced Chip Manufacturing.
🔗 Europe (e.g., Swiss/Netherlands): High-Precision Machines.

text:
I spent 2 hours of my Saturday reviewing hundreds of charts. These are the setups that stood out and what you should focus on this week.

Small caps are leading. Semiconductors are trying to reclaim leadership. Most of the Magnificent 7 still look tired.

$IWM closed at new highs.
$SMTC may be one of the cleanest setups on the board.
$SPCX enters its first full week as a public company.

Here’s the watchlist and recording:

$SPX: Strong bounce off the 50-day. Reclaimed 7335-7340 and closed above the prior Friday low. Above 7450 opens a move toward 7520. Below 7400 puts pressure back on the market.

$QQQ: Back near the highs. Above 724 keeps momentum intact and opens the door to a retest of all-time highs.

$IWM: One of the strongest charts right now. Closed at new highs around 293 and continues to lead.

$SMH: Semiconductors bounced hard and remain a key leadership group. Watching for continuation higher.

$BTC: Attempting to form a bottom, but still no clear setup. Needs more time.

$MSFT: Failed after the move toward the 200-day. No compelling setup here for now.

$META: Broke below 600 and continues to look vulnerable. Could see further downside if buyers don't step in soon.

$AMZN: Still holding above the 200-day. Watching 243 and 250 for signs of strength.

$AAPL: Clear bear flag developing. Relative weakness remains compared to the broader market.

$NVDA: Struggling below key moving averages. No clean setup at the moment.

$TSLA: Better than most mega caps. Closed near highs Friday. Watching how it behaves alongside $SPCX.

$GOOGL: Lower highs and below key moving averages. Needs a catalyst to regain momentum.

$SPCX: First full week as a public company. Watching the IPO range closely. Above the highs could trigger momentum. Below 150 would be a warning sign.

$ROKU: Huge move on strategic acquisition headlines. Above 150 keeps momentum alive.

$ARM: One of the stronger semiconductor charts. Holding 380 and breaking 385 could trigger another leg higher.

$AXTI: Photonics remains interesting. Watching 104 for continuation.

$INTC: One of the strongest semis on Friday. Above 128 could trigger a meaningful breakout.

$WDC: Filled the gap and recovered well. Watching for continuation above 572.

$SNDK: Excellent relative strength. Needs to reclaim and hold above 2000.

$MU: Lagging behind SNDK. Watching 1000 and 1015 closely.

$AMD: Strong bounce. Holding 500 and reclaiming 510-520 could open a move toward highs.

$NBIS: One of the more interesting setups. Above 240 could trigger fresh momentum.

$QCOM: Double-bottom style setup near range lows. Looking for rotation back toward highs.

CAVA: Quiet recovery underway. Looks capable of making a run toward 100.

$WOLF: Strong relative strength. Above 26.5 remains interesting.

$RL: New highs. Watching 400 hold and continuation above 405.

$AMAT: Clean continuation setup. Watching prior-day highs.

$GS: Improving again. Above 1080 opens the door to 1100.

$JPM: One of the stronger financial setups. Watching 322.

$SMTC: One of my favorite charts right now. Watching 170-173 for a breakout.

$BAC: Quietly approaching new highs.

$CAT: Rotation candidate if money moves away from tech. Watching 920-930.

$C: New highs and one of the strongest bank charts.

$HLT / $MAR: Hotels continue to show strength and sit near highs.

$LRCX: Strong trend remains intact. Watching above 370.

$HOOD: Strong recovery. Watching 95-96 for continuation.

$GE: Constructive hammer candle. Looks capable of making another run at highs.

$MRVL: One of the better semiconductor setups. Above 300 could accelerate quickly.

$UNH: Quiet strength. Watching continuation toward 440-445.

SNOW: Pulling back after a strong run. Needs time to reset.

$RKLB: Space trade cooled significantly after the $SPCX IPO. Needs stabilization.

$ASTS: Similar story. Heavy pullback after a huge run into the IPO.

Overall theme:

The market bounced, but confirmation is still needed.

IWM, semiconductors, memory names, and financials are showing the best relative strength.

Most of the Magnificent 7 continue to lag and remain below key moving averages.

SMTC, INTC, SNDK, NBIS, MRVL, SPCX, and JPM are some of my favorite charts going into next week.

Our strategy must be smarter. Our true competitive advantage is our massive digital market with ~13 Billion Monthly UPI Transactions processing ~$240 Billion USD.
​This frictionless economy generates behavioral data of unprecedented quality. This data is our bargaining chip in the new negotiation technology landscape. Our goal is not a self-sufficient ecosystem, but an irreplaceable one in the global value chain.
Strategic Alliances are Key: We must stop relying on the west and build a warehouse of specialized non-US allies.
​🔗 Japan: Optical Computing & Advanced Photonics.
🔗 South Korea: High-Density Data Center Comms & Power Highways.
🔗 Taiwan: Advanced Chip Manufacturing.
🔗 Europe (e.g., Swiss/Netherlands): High-Precision Machines.

text:
That is a very poor conclusion.
The central argument is that the US government’s strategy is fundamentally short-sighted. Ironically, American AI data centers would not function without European leadership in photonics. Anyone who has paid even modest attention to this sector should understand that by now.
But I don’t want to get drawn into the tired debate about who is dependent on whom.

As for whether a market of 449 million consumers matters, that is ultimately for business leaders to decide.

text:
Our strategy must be smarter. Our true competitive advantage is our massive digital market with ~13 Billion Monthly UPI Transactions processing ~$240 Billion USD.
​This frictionless economy generates behavioral data of unprecedented quality. This data is our bargaining chip in the new negotiation technology landscape. Our goal is not a self-sufficient ecosystem, but an irreplaceable one in the global value chain.
Strategic Alliances are Key: We must stop relying on the west and build a warehouse of specialized non-US allies.
​🔗 Japan: Optical Computing & Advanced Photonics.
🔗 South Korea: High-Density Data Center Comms & Power Highways.
🔗 Taiwan: Advanced Chip Manufacturing.
🔗 Europe (e.g., Swiss/Netherlands): High-Precision Machines.

text:
India’s AI Moat is NOT Sovereign Models. It’s Sovereign Data.

​As India rapidly digitizes, sophisticated AI-driven cyber attacks pose a threat to our critical infrastructure far beyond WhatsApp vulnerabilities. The era of trusting external tech partners is over.
​The prevailing wisdom is to build internal state capacity: sovereign models, native research. But the math doesn’t work. We lack the $300M+ GPU clusters and hyperscale data centers required for foundation LLM training.
​Our strategy must be smarter. Our true competitive advantage is our massive digital market with ~13 Billion Monthly UPI Transactions processing ~$240 Billion USD.
​This frictionless economy generates behavioral data of unprecedented quality. This data is our bargaining chip in the new negotiation technology landscape. Our goal is not a self-sufficient ecosystem, but an irreplaceable one in the global value chain.
​How we win: We must build a trust layer to monetize this data securely. At Equidei, our Zero-Knowledge Proof (ZK) based ML pipeline is designed for exactly this moment. By placing public hashes on a Distributed Ledger (DLT) with Smart Contracts, we enable trustless data sharing. Users can share insights derived from data, but no raw data access is granted, and no copies are made. This creates a secure "Federated Data" model.
​Strategic Alliances are Key: We must stop relying on the west and build a warehouse of specialized non-US allies.
​🔗 Japan: Optical Computing & Advanced Photonics.
🔗 South Korea: High-Density Data Center Comms & Power Highways.
🔗 Taiwan: Advanced Chip Manufacturing.
🔗 Europe (e.g., Swiss/Netherlands): High-Precision Machines.  
​India secures this Asia AI Warehouse by providing the one thing nobody else has: the massive behavioral data repository, verified and secured by a ZK-trust layer. This is how we build irreplacable capacity, secure our digital economy, and maintain true sovereignty.

LAYER 5: TEST AND THE ANALOG UNDERLAYER. Complexity is a tax paid in validation. 🔵 $AEHR: silicon photonics test, ramping with the cycle. ' 🔵 $VIAV: every 800G and 1.6T transceiver gets validated before it ships. The gate the market prices like an accessory. 🔵 $SMTC: the drivers and TIAs that fire the lasers. text:
I think one of the biggest underappreciated stories from the new EU Chips Act 2.0 isn't another mega fab. It's photonics. For years it felt like a niche research field. Now Europe is effectively treating it as strategic infrastructure. Why?

Because AI is running into a data movement problem. Copper is becoming the bottleneck, so the next wave is Co-Packaged Optics (CPO) and silicon photonics. That changes the investment story.

1) Soitec $SOI : The EU’s official impact analysis explicitly named Soitec, noting that "The EU has a relatively strong global position in SOI wafers, with Soitec... being notable players." Because optical transceivers and high-bandwidth optical interconnects are physically manufactured on Silicon Photonics-SOI (Silicon-on-Insulator) substrates, Soitec sits at the absolute genesis of the raw material supply chain.
In addition, Soitec is already deeply integrated into European microelectronics funding tracks (like the IPCEI ME/CT project), leading key workstreams in communications infrastructure. The Chips Act 2.0 ensures that Soitec's next-generation wafer expansion (like SmartSiC and specialized optical/RF substrates) will receive heavily subsidized structural runway.

2) X-FAB $XFAB is the pure-play specialty foundry leading the European Silicon Photonics and heterogeneous integration manufacturing value chain.
XFAB was also named in the impact analysis and qualifies for the prized FOAK (First-of-a-Kind) category. This means the company is positioned to receive streamlined state-aid approvals and direct EU grants to expand its European factories for next-generation silicon photonics modules.
$XFAB is the direct winner of the physical infrastructure side of Chips Act 2.0. The act’s specific focus on strengthening "production technologies including co-packaging and heterogeneous integration with electronic chips" is effectively a targeted mandate for what X-FAB does.

3) Sivers ($SIVE) is the higher-beta bet. 
The proposal explicitly highlights funding for "design libraries and design automation tools for photonic integrated circuits" and "co-packaged optics for AI data centres." Because Sivers operates a "fab-lite" model, the policy helps subsidize the complex R&D, design, and prototyping phases of their customer pipelines (e.g., Ayar Labs, Jabil, and Marvell). Furthermore, Sivers was recently integrated directly into GlobalFoundries’ ($GFS) SCALE™ CPO platform blueprints as a foundry reference design. Subsidizing European pilot lines for CPO means European systems builders will face lower friction adopting Sivers-backed laser architectures.
While the technology is deeply validated, $SIVE is in the middle of a brutal tug-of-war. Short-selling reports (e.g., Ningi Research), class-action shareholder lawsuits regarding listing delays, and heavy corporate insider selling have crushed governance credibility. 

So, the EU policy guarantees a robust, long-term industry tailwind for Sivers' technology, but near-term execution and corporate governance remain highly volatile.

As a forward-looking investor, I expect the market to price in these macro tailwinds well before physical cash flows reflect them. If Europe is serious about AI sovereignty, photonics is no longer optional. The market may not wait for revenues to show up before pricing that in.

We should expect *3 to 6 Months for  Legislative Passage & State-Aid Framework to finalized.
*Then 6 to 12 Months: Consortiums Formed and Direct EU Grants allocated
*Finally, 12 to 15+ Months: Revenue & Capital Expenditure expansion manifests.

Personally, I see:
• Lower-risk infrastructure: $SOI, $XFAB
• Higher-risk, higher-upside: $SIVE
The bottleneck is shifting from compute to connectivity. That's where I'm spending my research time.

text:
One of the BEST investment opportunities of the decade is unfolding right now. Most people still don’t see it.

Photonics is where Memory was a year ago. That’s why we’re still VERY early.

The pattern is clear if you’ve been watching this AI super cycle unfold since 2022.

> Chips kicked everything off after ChatGPT dropped in late 2022/early 2023. $NVDA went nuclear with Hopper H100s, and then Blackwell came. $AMD started carving out real share in GPUs and custom ASICs.

> Memory followed with a lag. Mid-2024 onward, HBM went into massive shortage as AI training and inference sucked up capacity. That pulled DRAM and NAND higher too. Micron $MU, SK Hynix, Samsung and other memory players delivered strong re-ratings as pricing power returned.

> Now in mid-2026, memory is deep in its prolonged supercycle - sold out for years ahead (confirmed by Micron CEO Sanjay Mehrotra). Chips are in a more mature phase, still growing strong with 1:1 CPU to GPU ratios as AI agents scale, and will sustain into the second half of this decade as Lisa Su says compute capacity will 100x by 2030.

But what’s next? 

Photonics, folks! 

- AI clusters are suffering from constraints related to power, heat, latency and bandwidth. Optics solves this. That is why the next MAJOR leg is clearly SiPh and CPO.

- 1.6T pluggables are ramping, early CPO deployments are starting but nowhere near to the scale they will reach because real mass adoption and deep in-package integration will come in 2027-2028+.

- Goldman Sach predicts TAM will explode 10x by the end of this decade. 

To better understand this, if one divides it into 3 common phases across the supercycles (Early, Acceleration, Maturity), we see that:

- Chips are clearly in Phase 3
- Memory is in Phase 2/3
- Photonics is very early in Phase 2 🔥

The AI buildout keeps shifting to the next bottleneck because the world needs more compute than ever before. This isn’t slowing down anytime soon!

BULLISH seems like a bearish word now.

The pattern is clear if you’ve been watching this AI super cycle unfold since 2022. > Chips kicked everything off after ChatGPT dropped in late 2022/early 2023. $NVDA went nuclear with Hopper H100s, and then Blackwell came. $AMD started carving out real share in GPUs and custom ASICs. > Memory followed with a lag. Mid-2024 onward, HBM went into massive shortage as AI training and inference sucked up capacity. That pulled DRAM and NAND higher too. Micron $MU, SK Hynix, Samsung and other memory players delivered strong re-ratings as pricing power returned. > Now in mid-2026, memory is deep in its prolonged supercycle - sold out for years ahead (confirmed by Micron CEO Sanjay Mehrotra). Chips are in a more mature phase, still growing strong with 1:1 CPU to GPU ratios as AI agents scale, and will sustain into the second half of this decade as Lisa Su says compute capacity will 100x by 2030. But what’s next? Photonics, folks! - AI clusters are suffering from constraints related to power, heat, latency and bandwidth. Optics solves this. That is why the next MAJOR leg is clearly SiPh and CPO. - 1.6T pluggables are ramping, early CPO deployments are starting but nowhere near to the scale they will reach because real mass adoption and deep in-package integration will come in 2027-2028+. - Goldman Sach predicts TAM will explode 10x by the end of this decade. To better understand this, if one divides it into 3 common phases across the supercycles (Early, Acceleration, Maturity), we see that: - Chips are clearly in Phase 3 - Memory is in Phase 2/3 - Photonics is very early in Phase 2 🔥 The AI buildout keeps shifting to the next bottleneck because the world needs more compute than ever before. This isn’t slowing down anytime soon!
